name: The Campbell & Fetter Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Northeastern Indiana since 1863, offering personal and business banking with conservative lending practices and locally-owned stability.
description_long:
Campbell & Fetter Bank was founded in 1863 by William Mitchell in Kendallville, Indiana, making it one of the oldest privately owned banks in the state. The institution has operated continuously through significant historical periods including the Civil War, two World Wars, the Great Depression, and multiple economic downturns. The bank changed ownership only once in 1976 when it transitioned from Mitchell's descendants. Over 160 years, it has built a reputation as a stable, locally-owned independent community bank.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, HSA accounts, personal loans, mortgages, ATM/debit cards, and credit cards with VISA Gift Card options. On the business side, they provide business checking and savings accounts, commercial loans, business credit cards, remote deposit capabilities, and tax payment services. Online services include NetTeller internet banking and mobile banking platforms. Loan applications are available online for auto/boat/RV/home equity loans, mortgages, and credit cards for both personal and business customers.

Campbell & Fetter distinguishes itself through its emphasis on conservative lending practices and local ownership in an increasingly consolidated banking landscape. The bank explicitly markets itself as "one of the most solid financial institutions in the state" and highlights three centuries of shareholder management as proof of stability. Their messaging prioritizes depositor safety and institutional longevity over competitive rate chasing, positioning themselves as a counter to national banking consolidation trends.

As a regional community bank, Campbell & Fetter likely serves its Northeastern Indiana market effectively for customers prioritizing relationship banking and local decision-making. However, the website provides minimal specific information about rates, fees, loan terms, or competitive advantages beyond stability claims. Prospective customers should contact them directly for rate comparisons and detailed product terms, as the digital presence is informational rather than comprehensive.
services: Personal loans (cars, boats, RVs, home equity)
pros: Founded in 1863 with 160+ year operating history through major economic crises, demonstrating institutional stability
Locally-owned independent community bank rather than national chain, enabling local decision-making
One of oldest privately owned banks in Indiana with only one ownership change in 160 years
Offers full-service banking including personal checking, savings, HSA, mortgages, and business accounts
Conservative lending practices explicitly stated as core philosophy, prioritizing depositor safety
Multiple service channels including online banking (NetTeller), mobile banking, and in-person locations
NMLS-regulated institution (NMLS# 416300) with established compliance framework
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Northeastern Indiana reduces accessibility for out-of-region customers
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, APRs, fees, or competitive product terms
No information provided about minimum balance requirements, account features, or loan approval criteria
Minimal digital innovation marketing; website content emphasizes history over modern banking convenience
No mention of mobile app features, API integrations, or advanced online banking tools
best_for: Northeastern Indiana residents and businesses seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers prioritizing bank stability and conservative lending over maximum interest rates
Small business owners needing local decision-making authority and personalized commercial services
Individuals seeking full-service banking (checking, savings, mortgages, loans) in one institution
diagnosis: Campbell & Fetter Bank is best for Northeastern Indiana residents and business owners who prioritize institutional stability, local ownership, and relationship banking over competitive rates and digital innovation. The primary caveat is limited geographic service area and minimal online rate/term transparency—customers should contact the bank directly for current pricing, as the website emphasizes heritage and stability rather than competitive product details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
